fix(snap): systemctl shim outputs state text for is-enabled/is-active

When a unit was not found in D-Bus, both is-enabled and is-active exited
silently with a non-zero code. execFileUtf8 then falls back to Node's
error message ('Command failed: /snap/.../systemctl --user is-enabled
openclaw-gateway.service') as the detail string, which does not match
any of the isSystemdUnitNotEnabled patterns, causing the caller to throw
instead of returning false.

Fix: emit the state word to stdout (matching real systemctl behaviour):
- is-enabled: 'not-found' + exit 4 when unit not in D-Bus
- is-active:  'inactive'  + exit 3 when unit not in D-Bus
- Both also echo the state when the unit IS found, mirroring systemctl

isSystemdUnitNotEnabled already includes 'not-found' in its pattern set,
so no TypeScript changes are needed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix: resolve vite-plugin-pwa peer dep with overrides

Adds npm overrides to force vite-plugin-pwa to use project vite version.
No more need for --legacy-peer-deps.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ubuclaw): skip loginctl linger check when running as snap

When running as a snap the gateway lifecycle is managed by snapd as a
user daemon (daemon-scope: user in snapcraft.yaml). loginctl is not
accessible from within the snap's confined environment, so
readSystemdUserLingerStatus() always returns null, producing the
misleading message 'Unable to read loginctl linger status. Ensure
systemd + loginctl are available.'

Snapd automatically manages lingering when the service is enabled via
`snap start --enable ubuclaw.service`, so the check is both unreachable
and unnecessary in this context.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ubuclaw): update import path for applyAgentDefaultModelPrimary

onboard-auth.config-shared.ts was removed upstream in refactor
'remove onboard auth compat barrels'. The function now lives in
src/plugins/provider-onboarding-config.ts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: use REST API instead of GraphQL for PR creation

'gh pr create' uses the GraphQL createPullRequest mutation which fails
with 'Resource not accessible by integration' on fork repos. Switch to
'gh api repos/.../pulls' (REST) and 'gh api .../issues/.../labels'
(REST) which do not have this restriction.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: drop new upstream workflows added by merge, not just conflicting ones

Switch from 'git merge --no-edit' (auto-commits) to 'git merge --no-commit'
so we can clean up before committing in all cases.

After the merge (clean or conflict-resolved), iterate .github/workflows/*.yml
and git-rm anything not in our whitelist (snap-build.yml, upstream-tag-watch.yml,
upstream-sync.yml). This prevents new workflows added by upstream from silently
landing in the fork on every sync.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: sanitize upstream_tag input and improve tag-not-found diagnostic

Strip accidental quotes/whitespace from manual workflow_dispatch input so
a stray trailing ' does not produce an invalid tag name. Also show only
v2026* tags in the diagnostic output (sorted by version) so the relevant
tags are visible without being buried by pagination.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: fix upstream-sync fetch - also fetch branches to get tag commit objects

'git fetch upstream --tags' fetches tag refs but the associated commit
objects may not be downloaded if no branch ref pulls them in. Adding a
plain 'git fetch upstream' first ensures all commit objects are present,
making the subsequent 'git merge <tag>' reliably work.

Also add a pre-merge check that fails fast with a clear error if the
resolved tag can't be found after fetch, instead of falling through into
the conflict handler with a misleading 'nothing to commit' error.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: fix upstream-sync YAML - use printf to avoid unindented heredoc content

Heredoc content at column 1 inside a YAML literal block scalar (run: |)
breaks the YAML parser. Replaced heredocs with printf format strings so
all content stays within the properly-indented block.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: fix upstream-sync conflict commit and label application

Two bugs fixed:
1. When src/ conflicts remain after auto-resolving our paths, the branch was
   pushed without a merge commit (HEAD was still at pre-merge). Fix: stage
   conflicted files with markers via 'git add' and commit them so the branch
   has real content for the PR diff.

2. 'gh pr create --label' was failing with 'label not found' even though the
   label exists. Fix: create the PR without --label, capture the PR number
   from the URL, then apply labels separately with 'gh pr edit --add-label'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: auto-resolve downstream-owned paths during upstream sync

When the upstream merge hits conflicts, automatically resolve files in
paths we always own (.github/, README.md, snap/, CHANGELOG.md):

- If the file exists in our HEAD: keep ours (handles content conflicts
  and upstream-modified-vs-our-modified cases).
- If we deleted it (modify/delete conflict): keep the deletion (e.g.
  workflows we removed stay removed after each upstream sync).

After auto-resolution, only flag as a conflict PR if unresolved
files remain (i.e. source file conflicts that need human review).
Otherwise commit the merge and open a clean auto-merge PR.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: fix upstream-sync label permissions and flag syntax

Add issues: write permission — GitHub labels use the issues API even
on PRs, so pull-requests: write alone is insufficient to apply them.

Also split --label "upstream-sync,conflict" into two separate --label
flags to avoid relying on gh CLI comma-splitting behavior.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: trim dependabot to GitHub Actions only

npm deps are managed by upstream sync — independent PRs would conflict.
Swift (macOS/iOS) and Gradle (Android) are not relevant to a snap-only
fork. Docker images are unused by the remaining workflows.

Only GitHub Actions updates are retained (checkout, snapcore actions),
changed to weekly since churn isn't needed. Removed the npm-npmjs
registry token entry (secret doesn't exist in this fork).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: simplify PR and issue templates for snap-only fork

- PR template: replace upstream scopes with snap/ubuclaw-relevant ones,
  simplify verification to Ubuntu+snap, remove heavy upstream process
  sections (security impact matrix, failure recovery, etc.)
- Bug report: snap-first fields (snap channel, Ubuntu version, snap
  version); redirect core OpenClaw bugs upstream; remove provider
  chain and multi-platform install fields
- Feature request: redirect core feature requests upstream; reframe
  as snap/Ubuntu-specific improvements with snap-relevant examples
- config.yml: replace OpenClaw Discord links with upstream issue
  tracker and Snapcraft docs links

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: fix upstream-tag-watch permissions and remove irrelevant workflows

- Add actions: write permission to upstream-tag-watch job so
  GITHUB_TOKEN can dispatch the upstream-sync workflow (fixes HTTP 403)
- Remove workflows not relevant to a snap-only fork:
  auto-response, stale, ci, codeql, sandbox-common-smoke,
  install-smoke, openclaw-npm-release, labeler, docker-release,
  workflow-sanity

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

CI(audit): ignore pygments CVE-2026-4539

Add pygments ReDoS vulnerability (CVE-2026-4539)
to audit ignore lists. Transitive dep via HA with
no fix version available yet.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Grimberg <tykeal@bardicgrove.org>

repository.description: Rental Control system for Home Assistant

commit.message:
feat(redesign): premium visual overhaul, number reconciliation, performance fix

Visual:
- Hero carousel: SVG chevron arrows (w-12 h-12), gradient CTA buttons
- Typography: eliminated all sub-10px text, minimum now text-xs (12px)
- Sub-pages: unique color-identity hero banners (cyan/mercado, green/tech, purple/pesquisa, red/seguranca)
- Panorama: larger KPIs, larger section headers, premium glass styling

Numbers reconciled:
- Countries: 972→37 (filter ≥20 jobs, exclude garbage)
- Insights: 104→46 (only publishable)
- Sources: 85→36 (real count)
- Removed fake 'Capital Rastreado US$ 1.4T' KPI (funding data is mis-sourced)
- Replaced with 'Tech Trends' KPI using real tech_trends data (38K signals)
- Fixed '~85% EUA' to '~54% EUA'
- Patents filtered: 37K mock-generated excluded

Methodology page:
- Replaced fake source list (CrunchBase, PitchBook, LinkedIn API) with real connected sources
- Added REAL/MOCK badges per KPI (10 real, 10 mock)
- Added honesty disclaimer about unconnected sources

Performance:
- Created materialized view mv_papers_vs_jobs (85s query→<100ms)
- Charts API now returns in <1s instead of timing out

Panorama charts:
- Replaced empty 'Investimento por Tipo' panel with 'Geopolítica ACLED' (929K events)

  models, policies, prompts, risk frameworks, assessments, approvals,
  telemetry, incidents, evidence, evaluations, releases, portfolio
  metrics, users, audit log, connectors, and landing zones

  auto-schema application via better-sqlite3

* Drop Python 3.8 support: incompatible with setuptools>=78.1.1

setuptools 78.x requires Python >= 3.9, so building for Python 3.8
fails after the setuptools minimum was raised to 78.1.1. Remove 3.8
from the CI test matrix and all release scripts (Linux, macOS, Windows),
and update the twine upload environments from py3.8 to py3.9.

---------

commit.message:
Add codex-instructions.md and install user instructions

Create codex-instructions.md as the Codex CLI equivalent of
copilot-instructions.md, with Azure DevOps section removed.
Both install.sh and install.ps1 now copy user-level instruction
files to ~/.copilot/ and ~/.codex/ during default installs.

- Add expandable install instructions to circuit-sim, elmer-fem, netlist-to-schematic READMEs
- Clarify ParaView is optional (GUI-only, not invoked programmatically)
- Update SKILL.md prerequisite sections for cross-platform clarity

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Polish README: fix grammar, jargon, passive voice, and descriptions

- 'not time to become' → 'who don't have time to become'
- 'items deployed' → 'everything deployed' (avoid Fabric jargon)
- 'the architecture' → 'an architecture' (13 task flows exist)
- Deploy row: passive → active voice to match other rows
- 'project-brief' → 'project brief' + em-dash for rhythm
- Item Registry: describe what the reader finds, not what the agent needs
- Templates: 'definition files' not 'starting points' (they're the real thing)

repository.name: fabric-task-flows
repository.description: Task flows let you describe your problem and have an AI agent generate your Microsoft Fabric solution—workspaces, pipelines, notebooks, data agents, models, reports, and more—delivered as a CI/CD‑ready script, ready to deploy.

commit.message:
Fix circuit-sim for Windows: text rawfile + encoding

- parse_rawfile.py: Support both binary and text (Values:) rawfile
  formats. Windows ngspice outputs text format even with -r flag.
- run_sim.py: Add encoding='utf-8' to all NamedTemporaryFile and
  Path.read_text() calls. Fixes UnicodeEncodeError on Windows
  where the default encoding is cp1252.

Tested: all three example netlists (rc_lowpass, pulse_response,
rc_sweep) pass end-to-end on Windows with ngspice 45.2.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Respect Retry-After header; add --verbose to publish/reconcile

- _get_retry_after() extracts Retry-After seconds from 429 responses;
  _retry_on_429 uses it when present, falls back to exponential backoff
- --verbose flag on publish and reconcile logs:
  - per-file sizes before upload (_log_upload_summary)
  - request timing and success/failure per HF API call
  - Retry-After header value (or 'not set') on 429s
- 8 new tests: Retry-After parsing, header-aware backoff, verbose
  output capture, CLI --verbose flag parsing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: stratum-hq
repository.description: Stratum-HQ is a high-fidelity human dataset enrichment engine. It transforms raw images into dense, multi-layered training data by integrating T5-Large text embeddings, DINOv3 visual and spatial tokens, and 133-point DWPose keypoints. Optimized for Sapiens and consumer GPUs, it bridges the gap between pixels and anatomical structure.

commit.message:
Add Windows/PowerShell cross-platform support

- Add install.ps1 for circuit-sim, elmer-fem, netlist-to-schematic
  (modeled after memory's existing install.ps1)
- Add top-level install.ps1 that iterates skills/*/install.ps1
- Add .gitattributes enforcing LF for .sh, CRLF for .ps1
- Update all READMEs with Windows PowerShell installation instructions
- Fix elmer-fem SKILL.md: replace sed/inline env var with cross-platform
- Fix memory SKILL.md: remove bash-specific mkdir -p and date syntax
- Fix AGENTS.md /tmp/ paths with relative paths (cross-platform)
- Add explicit encoding='utf-8' to run_sim.py Path.read_text()

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Rewrite README for a warmer first impression

- Lead with bold value prop instead of dry definition
- Add 'Who is this for?' section so readers self-identify
- Reframe skills table as benefits ('What it does for you')
- Move Quick Start below the pitch (why before how)
- Fold Item Registry and Templates into What's Inside table
- Consistent conversational tone throughout

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Deep scrub: dead code removal, doc fixes, skill efficiency, registry+signal updates

Wave 1 - Dead code & doc fixes:
- Remove orphaned $cli_version lines from sync-item-types.py
- Remove deprecated build_fab_commands() from registry_loader.py + tests
- Replace fragile importlib hack in fleet-runner.py with text_utils.slugify
- Fix stale counts in README (45 items, 838 tests), CONTRIBUTING, CHANGELOG
- Update fabric-test SKILL.md to 3-state validation (passed/partial/failed)

Wave 2 - Skill efficiency:
- Consolidate fabric-discover Steps 4/5 to eliminate duplicate user Q&A
- Add parallel-read instruction to fabric-document Step 1

Wave 3 - Registry & signal mapper:
- Add Scorecard item type to registry (45 total items)
- Patch signal-categories.json with 8 new keywords across 3 categories
- Add low-coverage advisory to signal-mapper.py output
- Align task type names in taskflow-gen.py, tests, and schema (ingest/store/etc)
- Fix test-plan-prefill.py availability check to match registry abbreviation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix(checkin): track events by identity key

Replace position-0 event lookups with identity-key
search in CHECKED_IN and CHECKED_OUT state handlers.
Prevents oscillation between states on coordinator
refresh and ensures tracked events survive list
reordering. Add _find_tracked_event helper. Guard
linger recomputation with follow-on event key.

Add tests for _find_tracked_event, tracked event at
non-zero position, oscillation guard, same-day
turnover flow, and linger follow-on key management.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Grimberg <tykeal@bardicgrove.org>
